generic_rules: Repairs should focus on fixing AGREE syntax errors without altering the underlying AADL architecture.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE type declarations (int, real, bool) match the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` helpers to define reusable expressions within AGREE contracts.
Range checks can be expressed as `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
anti_patterns: Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or dependencies not present in the AADL model.
Do not attempt to fix type mismatches by modifying the AADL architecture.
Do not add architecture to resolve AGREE errors; focus on correcting the contract itself.
